sutta: 168. now the verbal maha mauglana went to visit the venerable sariputta et cetera et cetera and said this to him sariputta your reverence there are these 4 modes of progress as before now your reverence by way of which of these 4 was your heart released from the asavas without grasping and venable sariputta answered mughlana your reference there are as you say these 4 modes of progress now these 4 the 1 i followed was the mode of progress that is pleasant with swift higher knowledge by way of this mode of progress was my ma was my heart released from the asavas without grasping
commentary:
so in this suta you find that the verb sariputta he had what we say stronger paramis stronger better higher um perfections of character so that he was not very much disturbed by lust hatred and delusion and so his progress was pleasant and because he made great effort now it was also very swift now we come to sutra number 4 point by 169 this is quite an important suta monks these 4 persons are found existing in the world what for here in monks a certain person in this very life is set free but with some effort again another person is set free when body breaks up with some effort herein again monks a certain person is set free in this very life but without effort yet again another person is set free when body breaks up without effort now monks how is a person 1 who in this very life is set free but with some effort in this case among lives contemplating the loadsomeness of body aware of the repulsiveness of food aware of his distaste for all the world aware of impermanence in all the conditioners sankaras thus awareness of death is well implanted in the very self he lives dependent on these 5 powers of a pupil the power of faith that of conscience that means the fear to do wrong remorse means a sense of shame of having done wrong and energy and the power of wisdom in him these 5 controlling faculties are also manifested in abundance namely the controlling faculty of faith energy recollection or mindfulness concentration and wisdom thus by the preponderance of these 5 controlling faculties in this very life he is set free but with some effort and how monks is a person again set free when body breaks up with some effort in this case among lives contemplating the lonesomeness of body aware of the repulsiveness of food aware of his distaste for all the world aware of impermanence in all the conditioners thus awareness of death is well planted in the very self he lives dependent on these 5 powers of a pupil namely the power of faith conscience remorse energy wisdom but in him these 5 controlling faculties are duly manifested namely the controlling faculty of faith energy recollection or mindfulness concentration and wisdom thus by the dullness of these faculties he is set free but with some effort when body breaks up and how monks is a person set free in this very life but without effort in this case among alluve from sen's desires etc enters and abides in the first jhana the second jana third jannah fourth jhana he lives dependent on these 5 powers of a pupil that is faith conscience remorse energy and wisdom in him the 5 controlling faculties are manifested in abundance that is faith energy recollection uh concentration wisdom thus by the pre-ponderance of these 5 controlling faculties he is set free in this very life without effort and how monks is a person again 1 who is set free without effort when body breaks up in this case among allu from sen's desires etc enters on the 4 janas and abides therein he lives dependent on the 5 powers of a pupil but in him the 5 controlling faculties are duly manifested owing to the dullness of these 5 faculties it is not till body breaks up that he is set free without effort so these are the 4 persons found existing in the world ah here this is about the arahants how they attain our handhood so here we find there are different conditions there is 1 who is set free and before he is dying he attains arahantha before he dies and it can be with effort or it can be without effort and then there's another type who only attains on his deathbed when he is dying and then also with effort or without effort and then we find the first 1 the 1 who is set free before death with some effort he does not have the 4 ghanas but the 5 faculties are strong because the 5 faculties 5 controlling faculties are strong then he is set free before death the second 1 is set free at death but with some effort with effort also he does not have the 4 genres but he has the 5 faculties in a quite weak his 5 faculties are weak and then he set free only when and dying at the time of dying and the third 1 is set free before death but without effort and this 1 possesses the 4 janas and the faculties are also very strong and the last 1 is set free only at the death and without effort he possesses the 4 janas but the faculties are weak so when a person possesses the 4 jaanas you can see from here that he attains without effort so with the 4 janas he attains arahan hood without effort whereas without the 4 janas he needs effort but this does not mean that he does not have any jana because in all these cases he has the 5 faculties and in the 5 faculties you'll notice the 5 calculates of faith energy recollection or mindfulness concentration and wisdom so 1 of the faculties is concentration and concentration perfect concentration even for the faculties are the 4 janas so when you say that the faculties are weak it means probably that he has only 1 jana 1 jana or 2 janas probably 1 and then when the faculties are strong probably he has 2 or 3 janas 2 or 3 jhanas whereas when the when the faculties are perfect then he has all the 4 janas because in the uh earliest we read that and arahan and anagami must possess the jhanas to attain anagami and arahantha so that's about the the janus and then you notice that all of them are they they are they contemplate the loadsomeness of the body aware of the repulsiveness of food aware of the distaste for the world aware of impermanence in all the conditioners thus awareness of death is well planted in the very self so this awareness of death is 1 thing that is very important if we practice the holy life we should always contemplate death always remember that death is very near any time we can pass away firstly it gives us a sense of urgency and secondly yeah because of that as a result of that now we are more mindful and we we our subtitle concentration and concentration is very important for the destruction of the asavasa so that's the 1 thing you have to remember are these few things rudiments of the body repulsiveness of food distaste for the world impermanence in all the conditioners all the conditioners and the conditioned things then awareness of death then the second thing we want to note is the 5 powers of a pupil uh pupil is 1 who is cultivating the holy life and these 5 powers of a people are faith faith is quite important that means having trust or confidence in the buddha's teachings especially here normally we say in the triple gem basically it is the dhamma and then conscience conscience is the fear to do wrong that means before you you do any wrong you have that fear of doing wrong and then that is hearing then the third 1 is remorse remorse is a sense of shame a sense of shame if we have done anything wrong we regret it that is now these 2 things conscience and remorse they're quite important qualities for a person on the spiritual path and then the energy and wisdom these are the 5 powers of a people then the 5 controlling faculties uh faith energy recollection or mindfulness concentration and wisdom these 5 things are also very important these are part of the 37 bodhi pakiya dharmas and the buddha said now that when the noble eightfold path is perfected all the 37 bodhipakya dharmas are perfected the faculties the powers the bojanga the rna4 path etc so you can see that for liberation in this life these few things are important the constant contemplation of death then the 5 powers of a pupil the 5 controlling faculties and the janus if you have the jhanas then we don't need so much effort but then we have seen in some earlier suta that the jhanas are important if you want to attain anagami and some people because we find attaining the janas are so difficult then we can concentrate on listening to the sutas because the suta panna and we don't need the janas and in the sutas we find that a lot of people enter the stream from listening to the sutras so it is good now we put more effort now on learning the sutas once we have this foundation later we can concentrate on attaining concentration
